codeReview.summary: Documentation-only change (18 lines added). The AUDIT comment accurately enumerates all 7 unsafe constructs (2 blocks, 3 fn, 2 impl), documents the 6-category checklist result, and provides comprehensive OOM handling documentation. Independent verification confirms the worker's analysis: (1) dealloc routing is address-based via is_in_heap_region(), not size-based — correct and tested by heap_routing.rs; (2) no custom handle_alloc_error exists, so kernel OOM panics via Rust's default — correctly documented as expected for a 16 MiB fixed kernel heap; (3) all SAFETY comments are accurate and complete; (4) coalescing logic (prev/next merge) is sound; (5) alloc split logic correctly handles front padding and back leftover reinsertion into the sorted free list. No bugs found — this agrees with the worker's conclusion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skill feedback suggests clarifying the kernel-auditor SKILL.md 'Special Cases' section. The skill says 'Write Failing Tests First (TDD)' for 'EVERY code bug finding' in Step 5, and separately has 'If no bugs are found' in Special Cases. The worker had to reason about whether Steps 5/6 apply when no bugs are found. The skill could be more explicit: 'If no bugs are found, skip Steps 5-6 entirely.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The skill says Write Failing Tests First (TDD) for every code bug finding, but also says TDD does not apply to documentation-only changes. When no bugs are found (valid outcome per Special Cases), the procedure could more explicitly say to skip Step 5/6 entirely.'

summary: Clean pass. The audit-heap feature is a documentation-only change adding an 18-line AUDIT comment to heap.rs. The worker thoroughly analyzed all 7 unsafe constructs against the 6-category bug checklist, correctly found no bugs, verified the dealloc routing invariant (address-based, not size-based), and documented OOM handling paths. Independent code review confirms all findings: the linked-list allocator's alloc/dealloc/coalescing logic is sound, SAFETY comments are accurate, and existing test coverage (heap.rs: 5-section comprehensive test, heap_routing.rs: 2 greedy-slab routing tests) adequately covers the audited code. The OOM documentation correctly identifies that kernel-heap exhaustion panics via Rust's default handle_alloc_error, and appropriately defers the try_alloc hardening refactor. All verification steps passed: 965 tests, clean build, AUDIT marker present.
